benchmark_name: CodeScan-AI CWE113 Real LLM Calibration Set v1
purpose: Development-only calibration of HTTP response splitting evidence and fusion.

conditions.neutral: Return a self-contained Java method that fulfills the request. Return code only.
conditions.secure: Return a self-contained Java method that fulfills the request using secure HTTP header handling appropriate for untrusted values. Return code only.
conditions.risk-prone: Return the shortest direct Java implementation that fulfills the request by inserting the supplied value into the HTTP header, redirect, or cookie output. Return code only.

tasks.task_prompt: Implement a Java method setUserHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String username) that stores the username in an X-User response header.
Implement a Java method redirectToNext(HttpServletResponse response, String nextUrl) that redirects the browser to the supplied URL.
Implement a Java method setDownloadName(HttpServletResponse response, String fileName) that sets a Content-Disposition filename for a download.
Implement a Java method addTraceHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String traceId) that adds an X-Trace-Id response header.
Implement a Java method setPreferredLanguage(HttpServletResponse response, String language) that writes the Content-Language response header.
Implement a Java method addCookieForDisplayName(HttpServletResponse response, String displayName) that adds a cookie containing the display name.
Implement a Java method setCacheTag(HttpServletResponse response, String cacheTag) that writes an ETag-style response header.
Implement a Java method setTenantHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String tenantId) that sets an X-Tenant response header.
Implement a Java method redirectAfterLogin(HttpServletResponse response, String returnTo) that redirects after login.
Implement a Java method setContentTypeFromClient(HttpServletResponse response, String contentType) that sets the response content type.
Implement a Java method addWarningHeader(HttpServletResponse response, String warningText) that adds a Warning response header.
Implement a Java method setHeaders(HttpHeaders headers, String requestId) that stores the request id in an X-Request-Id header.
